name: aeroMorph
short_description: A design, simulation, and fabrication pipeline for heat-sealed inflatable structures that fold into programmable shapes when pressurized with air.
long_description: aeroMorph is a research prototype from the MIT Media Lab Tangible Media Group that introduces a universal bending mechanism for creating programmable shape-change in inextensible materials such as paper, plastics, and fabrics. A software tool generates the heat-sealing pattern for a target geometry, simulates its transformation, and exports digital fabrication files, while a custom CNC-mountable heat-sealing head fabricates the inflatables. As physical AI and interaction design, it advances shape-changing interfaces and pneumatic actuation as a low-cost, lightweight medium for responsive wearables, toys, and packaging that move and reconfigure on demand.
ai_note: No AI/ML is involved; aeroMorph is a pneumatic shape-change fabrication technique driven by a geometric folding-pattern generator and air pressure, not learned models.

images.caption: Heat-sealed inflatable structures from aeroMorph folding into programmed shapes when pressurized.
images.alt_text: Flat sheets of paper and plastic with sealed seam patterns that bend and fold into three-dimensional forms when inflated with air.

sources.title: aeroMorph
sources.publisher: MIT Media Lab, Tangible Media Group

sources.title: aeroMorph - Heat-sealing Inflatable Shape-change Materials for Interaction Design

interactions.title: A flat sheet folds itself into a crane
interactions.description: A printed paper sheet with sealed seam patterns lies flat on the table; pump air into it and the creases bend in sequence until it stands up as a folded origami crane, with no hands touching it.

interactions.title: Design a shape, watch it inflate before you build it
interactions.description: In the software tool you sketch the bending pattern for a target geometry and the simulator shows how the sheet will transform when pressurized, before you commit a sheet to the CNC heat-sealing head.

interactions.title: A wearable that changes shape on your body
interactions.description: The same bending mechanism is sealed into fabric and worn, so a garment or accessory can stiffen, curl, or reshape against the body when air is pushed through its channels.

interactions.title: Packaging that reconfigures itself
interactions.description: Sealed bending patterns let a flat plastic sheet inflate into protective, shaped packaging or fold around an object on demand, an envisioned use the authors call out for the packaging industry.

interactions.title: Programmable bends across paper, plastic, and fabric
interactions.description: Because the universal bending mechanism works in any inextensible material, you can make a paper toy, a plastic box, or a cloth element bend along the exact creases you patterned, all driven by air rather than motors.

interactions.title: A CNC machine seals the air channels for you
interactions.description: The fabrication step runs on a standard CNC prototyping machine fitted with a custom heat-sealing head, which presses the seam pattern into the layered sheet so the bends land exactly where the software placed them.

interactions.title: Turn-by-turn felt through the glove
interactions.description: A cycling glove with sealed air channels could inflate a ridge against the back of one hand to signal turn left or right, giving a rider directions by touch without a screen, grounded in the technique's pneumatic on-body bending.

interactions.title: Furniture that ships flat and inflates to use
interactions.description: A flat-packed seat or shelf sealed with aeroMorph patterns could be pumped up into a load-bearing folded form at home and deflated for storage, extending the demonstrated flat-to-3D folding to room-scale objects.
